comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "landed on main via Gerrit (#143/#144/#163/#164, merged); plan-review is moot for already-merged code — closing to run the completion-verifier gate".

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Non-structural findings (coherence and other plan-semantics criteria) re-litigate closed children
too, and carry no `_container_child`. Without a finding<->closed-child association we cannot tell a
live signal from noise, and cannot distinguish plan-semantics from delivered-functionality.

## What

Add a SEPARATE Pass-2 sub-call `plan_review_completion` in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py`,
mirroring the `plan_review_novelty` sub-call (its own contract + prompt), that ALONE receives the
delivered-children manifest (their own AC text) and emits, per finding, three atomic sub-answers:
ATTRIBUTION (closed child id or none; structural pass-through for G3/G4 `_container_child`),
CONTAINMENT (`limited-to-closed | spans-open-or-system | n/a`), LAYER
(`plan-semantics | delivered-functionality | n/a`). Write the rubric prompt at
`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_completion.md`, register the contract, and add it to the
derived prompt index. Bounded/validated outputs; every ambiguous value fails toward keep. Frozen
wording (not regenerated per run) per G-Eval guidance.

## Scope

- IN: the sub-call + contract registration + the prompt + output parsing/validation + prompt-index
  entry.
- OUT: the Pass-3 drop decision (separate story); the gold-set calibration (separate story).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A `plan_review_completion` sub-call exists, registered like `plan_review_novelty`, receiving ONLY the delivered-children manifest (Pass-1 independence preserved).
- [ ] Per finding it emits {attribution, containment, layer} with the closed enums above; malformed/insufficient answers coerce to the keep-safe value.
- [ ] G3/G4 attribution is taken structurally from `_container_child` (not re-asked); a None/unattributed finding is never force-attributed.
- [ ] The rubric prompt `plan_review_completion.md` exists, is in the prompt index, and passes the CI drift gate; wording is frozen.
- [ ] Unit tests cover parsing + the fail-safe coercions for each sub-answer.

## Sub-call contract (fully specified)

Closed enums (each a closed set; fail-safe value in brackets):
- `attribution`: `<closed-child-ticket-id>` | `none` [none]. `none` = not about any closed child
  (open child, the container itself, or cross-cutting). For G3/G4 findings the value is taken
  STRUCTURALLY from `_container_child` (deterministic, not LLM-asked); for all other criteria it is
  LLM-emitted and VALIDATED against the delivered-children id set (an id not in the set -> none).
- `containment`: `limited-to-closed` | `spans-open-or-system` | `n-a` [spans-open-or-system].
- `layer`: `plan-semantics` | `delivered-functionality` | `n-a` [delivered-functionality].
Only `containment=limited-to-closed` + `layer=plan-semantics` + a delivered-now attribution can
enable a drop; every other value keeps.

Output contract: a `CompletionSubAnswers` model {attribution:str, containment:Literal[...],
layer:Literal[...]} per finding, registered as the `plan_review_completion` contract via
`register_contracts()` (mirroring `novelty_model` -> `plan_review_novelty`, passes.py:147), prompt
`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_completion.md`.

Determinism: Pass-3 is deterministic GIVEN the sub-answers — attribution is deterministic for
container-coverage findings (`_container_child`) and LLM-emitted+validated otherwise, exactly as
Pass-2 severity is LLM-emitted while Pass-3 decide is deterministic.

Manifest assembly (OWNER: this story, in `orchestrator.py`): iterate the epic's children, call
`delivered_now` per child, collect each delivered child's own AC text into the delivered-children
manifest handed to the sub-call (the sub-call ALONE receives it, mirroring novelty's prior-findings).

Degrade path: total sub-call failure (LLM error / parse failure / timeout) -> emit NO completion
sub-answers -> the completion floor drops NOTHING that run (all kept), mirroring `novelty()`==0.0
(carryover, never dropped). Fail toward keep.

## Acceptance Criteria (contract hardening)

- [ ] Each of `attribution` / `containment` / `layer` is a CLOSED enum exactly as listed above; the parser coerces any unknown/missing value to the bracketed fail-safe (which keeps).
- [ ] `containment=limited-to-closed` is the exact value the Pass-3 floor checks (no `limited` vs `limited-to-closed` drift across stories).
- [ ] The `plan_review_completion` contract + `CompletionSubAnswers` model + prompt file exist and are registered like `plan_review_novelty`; a test asserts registration.
- [ ] The delivered-children manifest assembler (in `orchestrator.py`) is implemented and unit-tested; the sub-call receives ONLY that manifest (Pass-1 independence).
- [ ] Total sub-call failure degrades to zero drops (floor inert that run), with a test.

file_impact.reason: plan_review_completion Pass-2 sub-call + CompletionSubAnswers contract (mirror novelty)
assemble the delivered-children manifest (iterate children, call delivered_now, collect AC text)
new rubric prompt: attribution/containment/layer closed-enum sub-questions
register/route the completion sub-call in the derived prompt index

verify_commands.dd_text: sub-call emits the three closed-enum sub-answers per finding
